ArchitectureNotes.objective: Package the existing Guardian plugin without behavior drift, while adding a small CLI that manages portable OpenCode config safely.
ArchitectureNotes.non-goal: Do not expand into a large monorepo or broad profile manager before the first plugin packaging path is stable.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-1: Config file mutation must stay conservative because users may already have hand-maintained JSONC with comments.

contract.purpose: Resolve OpenCode config paths, edit JSONC safely, render managed Guardian config, and inspect installation state.

export-resolvePaths.PURPOSE: Resolve config and Guardian file paths for the selected scope.

export-ensurePackageInstalled.PURPOSE: Ensure the npm plugin is registered in OpenCode config.

export-syncGuardianConfig.PURPOSE: Rewrite managed Guardian config while preserving current values.

notes.note-1: Main config edits should preserve comments when only the plugin array changes.

contract.purpose: Expose install, sync, status, doctor, and Guardian config subcommands through citty.

export-install.PURPOSE: Bootstrap plugin registration and optional Guardian config.

export-doctor.PURPOSE: Diagnose installation drift or invalid config.

notes.note-1: Commands should stay thin and delegate file logic to `M-CLI-CONFIG`.

contract.purpose: Review OpenCode permission requests with a constrained Guardian agent and auto-allow only low-risk actions.

export-GuardianPlugin.PURPOSE: Register Guardian hooks, config agent, and permission review flow.

notes.note-1: This module is a direct port of the existing standalone plugin and should change minimally at first.

DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.NAME: PluginInstall
DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.step-1: Resolve scope-specific OpenCode and Guardian config paths.
DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.step-2: Ensure `@osovv/vv-opencode` exists in the OpenCode `plugin` array.
DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.step-3: Create `guardian.jsonc` when it does not already exist.
DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.evidence: `vvoc status`, parsed config content, and successful typecheck/build.

DF-CONFIG-SYNC.NAME: ManagedConfigSync
DF-CONFIG-SYNC.step-1: Inspect whether Guardian config is CLI-managed.
DF-CONFIG-SYNC.step-2: Parse current Guardian values from JSONC.
DF-CONFIG-SYNC.step-3: Rewrite managed file canonically while preserving current values.
DF-CONFIG-SYNC.evidence: Stable rewritten `guardian.jsonc` and no overwrite of unmanaged files without force.

DF-GUARDIAN-SETTINGS.NAME: GuardianConfigWrite
DF-GUARDIAN-SETTINGS.step-1: Parse CLI overrides for model, variant, timeout, and thresholds.
DF-GUARDIAN-SETTINGS.step-2: Render a managed Guardian config template.
DF-GUARDIAN-SETTINGS.step-3: Write or print the config depending on mode.
DF-GUARDIAN-SETTINGS.evidence: JSONC parse succeeds and plugin can load the written file later.

DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.NAME: GuardianPermissionReview
DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.step-1: Capture relevant tool and command intent for the session.
DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.step-2: Build a bounded transcript and planned action prompt.
DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.step-3: Run Guardian, interpret risk, and auto-reply only when below threshold.
DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.evidence: Guardian logs, toasts, and permission reply behavior inside OpenCode.

Phase-1.name: CLI Foundation
Phase-1.goal: Establish safe config mutation helpers and wire them into a usable Bun CLI.
Phase-1.step-1: Implement path resolution, JSONC edits, inspection, and Guardian template helpers.
Phase-1.step-2: Expose install, sync, status, doctor, and Guardian config commands.

Phase-2.name: Guardian Packaging
Phase-2.goal: Move the existing standalone Guardian plugin into the repository and export it from the package.
Phase-2.step-1: Port the current Guardian plugin source into `src/plugins/guardian.ts`.

ExecutionPolicy.worker-owns: Only package source files, tests, and narrowly scoped documentation updates required by the approved module changes.
